title: Claude TDD Pro v1.9 — Source-Folder File
description: Schema for a single YAML file under generated-code-quality-standards/<namespace>/. Per architectural design v1.9 §G-2 / §2.21. Each file is a self-contained, ESLint-style rule configuration block sourced from one upstream publication. Cross-validation (rules conform to rubric-rule.schema.json; recommended_set ⊆ all_set; all_set == set of rule IDs in rules:) is performed by validate-source-file.sh in addition to this structural schema.

id.description: Source ID matching an entry in the appropriate operator-curated registry (STANDARDS-URLS.yaml, COMPLIANCE-URLS.yaml, or PR-SOURCES.yaml).

authoritative_publisher.description: Publishing body name (e.g., 'Google LLC', 'U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology', 'OWASP Foundation', 'Consumer Financial Protection Bureau').

authoritative_url.description: Live published URL of the upstream source.

registry_link.description: Which operator-curated registry this source is registered in.

registry_entry_id.description: Optional explicit pointer to the registry entry id (typically equals `id`).

fetched_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last successful fetch. Live freshness gate (S-13/S-16) consumes this.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of normalized source content at fetch time. Lock file pins this.

fetch_frequency.description: Per-source fetch cadence. Default: daily for engineering standards; weekly for compliance frameworks; daily for PR-corpus.

fragility_tier.description: Per-tier fetcher behavior: high → silent replace; medium → prompt on >5% structure delta; low → manual fetch only.

license_note.description: License + citation guidance for content quoted from this source.

rules.description: Array of rubric rule objects. Each rule MUST conform to schemas/rubric-rule.schema.json — cross-validation enforced by validate-source-file.sh.

items.description: Rubric rule per schemas/rubric-rule.schema.json. Structural validation deferred to that schema; this schema only checks the wrapper requires `id` so cross-validation can pair rules to all_set.

recommended_set.description: Subset of rule IDs included in this file's recommended subset. Cross-validation: MUST be subset of all_set.

all_set.description: Every rule ID defined in this file. Cross-validation: MUST equal exactly the set of `id` values in `rules:`.

notes.description: Optional operator-readable description of this file's scope.

migration_history.description: Optional record of prior file paths if rules were moved here.
